sections.subtitle: Recent Form Analysis
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano have had a mixed run in their last 5 matches with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, showing inconsistency but capability to score. Celta Vigo, on the other hand, have drawn 4 of their last 5 matches, indicating defensive resilience but a lack of finishing power with only 4 goals scored in those games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head encounters, Rayo Vallecano have won both matches (2-1 at home and 2-1 away), suggesting a slight edge over Celta Vigo in recent meetings. Both games were competitive with goals from both sides.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics Overview
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano rank higher in creating big chances (10, league rank 7) and penalty area entries (30.75 per game, rank 7), indicating attacking intent at home. Celta Vigo struggle to convert chances (4 goals from 6.6549 xG), which aligns with their inability to secure wins this season.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano's home advantage at Estadio de Vallecas and in-form player Álvaro García (3 goals in last 5 matches) could be decisive. Celta Vigo's tendency to concede first (4/5 matches) and lack of clean sheets (5/5 matches) might hurt their chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Rayo Vallecano and Celta Vigo ended in a goalless draw at Estadio de Vallecas. Neither side managed to break the deadlock despite some promising moments. Rayo Vallecano dominated possession and showed greater control in the final third, while Celta Vigo had a more dangerous edge in attack at times, creating a notable opportunity but failing to convert.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Rayo Vallecano's performance
halftimeSections.text: Rayo Vallecano controlled much of the game with superior ball possession and frequent entries into the final third. Their ability to win duels, especially in the air, stood out as a key strength. However, their attacking output lacked the necessary sharpness, with no shots on target despite several entries into the penalty area. Players like Sergio Camello and Jorge de Frutos showed creativity with key passes, but the final touch was missing. Defensively, they were solid, with Florian Lejeune leading clearances to neutralize Celta’s threats.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Celta Vigo's performance
halftimeSections.text: Celta Vigo, while having less of the ball, posed a more direct threat in the first half. They created a big chance but squandered it, highlighting a lack of clinical finishing. Borja Iglesias was central to their attacking efforts, getting a shot on target, though his impact was limited by Rayo’s strong defensive structure. Their struggle in aerial duels and overall duels was evident, which hampered their ability to maintain consistent pressure. Defensively, players like Yoel Lago contributed with clearances to keep the score level.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half swung between the two teams at various stages. Rayo Vallecano started with strong intent, maintaining consistent attacking spells in the early minutes. Celta Vigo responded with a period of pressure around the middle of the half, showing intent to exploit gaps. Rayo regained dominance later, with several phases of sustained attacking play, particularly towards the end of the half. Celta had their moments too, with a few notable attacking surges, but neither side could translate momentum into goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Rayo Vallecano will likely continue to leverage their possession and final third presence to create more clear-cut chances. They need to be more incisive in front of goal, potentially relying on players like Sergio Camello to unlock Celta’s defense. Celta Vigo, on the other hand, will aim to capitalize on counter-attacks or set pieces, with Borja Iglesias remaining a key figure in their pursuit of a breakthrough. Both teams are expected to push harder, with Rayo having a slight edge due to their control of the game so far.
